title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Hakaste and Detective Blue (Colin) discuss a witness who claimed to see a shadowy figure in the garden of number 19, though Hakaste dismisses the claim as unreliable. Hakaste also expresses suspicion toward Mrs. Black, noting her strange lack of awareness regarding her neighbor, Miss Pema, and her indifference toward the murder case.

Upon returning to the station, Colin informs Hakaste that he is leaving for London to provide a report and intends to consult his father's friend, the private detective Hercule Poirot, for inspiration. Hakaste expresses surprise, as he believed Poirot was dead.

Meanwhile, Hercule Poirot reviews daily files and receives a report from Sergeant Grey stating that no one at transit hubs recognized the photograph of Mr. Kuri. Laboratory analysis of a suit reveals that the tailor's label was removed, leaving it unclear if Mr. Kuri or the killer intended to hide an identity. Poirot notes that the deceased's dental records are the most promising lead for identification.

Hakaste continues his investigation, noting that Ms. Sheila Webber had an appointment with Professor Puddy at the Heron Hotel. While walking toward 14 Palmerston Road to visit Mrs. Rudd—the aunt of a person involved in the case—Hakaste encounters a vaguely familiar girl with blue eyes and fair skin. At the residence, he meets the disheveled Mrs. Rudd, who expresses frustration with reporters. Hakaste enters her home and hands her several envelopes he picked up from the doormat.
